comments.comment: The Silvertip Mine was in Care and Maintenance starting 19 February 2020.  The facility continues to be in care and maintenance; however, staff began arriving back to site in fall (September/October 2020).

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Less PM emitted in the form of road dust due to decrease in the average weight of the mine fleet.

Point sources or stack emissions at the facility emit particulate matter as a result of material handling (e.g., conveying, material handling, etc.), combustion of fuels (i.e., propane, diesel and natural gas) and burning (incineration and open burning).

For combustion of diesel, the emission factors used do not differentiate between condensable and filterable particulates; therefore, it is unknown if these particulate estimates include condensable particulates.  For all other sources and activities only filterable particulates are provided.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Less PM-10 emitted in the form of road dust due to decrease in the average weight of the mine fleet.

Point sources or stack emissions at the facility emit particulate matter as a result of material handling (e.g., conveying, material handling, etc.), combustion of fuels (i.e., propane, diesel and natural gas) and burning (incineration and open burning).

For combustion of diesel, the emission factors used do not differentiate between condensable and filterable particulates; therefore, it is unknown if these particulate estimates include condensable particulates.  For all other sources and activities only filterable particulates are provided.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
Less PM-2.5 emitted in the form of road dust due to decrease in the average weight of the mine fleet.

Point sources or stack emissions at the facility emit particulate matter as a result of material handling (e.g., conveying, material handling, etc.), combustion of fuels (i.e., propane, diesel and natural gas) and burning (incineration and open burning).

For combustion of diesel, the emission factors used do not differentiate between condensable and filterable particulates; therefore, it is unknown if these particulate estimates include condensable particulates.  For all other sources and activities only filterable particulates are provided.
